subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: On-site wastewater management systems must protect public health by ensuring that—

ol.li: risks associated with the discharge of treated wastewater and/or the end product from a composting toilet to the environment are minimised; and
foul air and gasses are prevented from accumulating within or entering into buildings; and
the likelihood of contamination of the drinking water supply is avoided.

callout.p: Wastewater contains a range of pathogens that can cause illness in humans. A well designed, maintained, and operated wastewater system improves sanitation and reduces the risk to public and environmental health. Poorly designed and maintained wastewater systems can result in, contamination of drinking water and recreational water sources, including those used for food production, as well as increased risks of direct wastewater contact by the public.
